items.title: Spot Prices Wrap: Natural Gas Cash Called Lower as Weather Demand Eases
items.summary: Physical gas prices progressed in some regions, but relatively steep losses in parts of the Midwest and East weighed down the national average on Wednesday.

items.title: U.S. LNG Feed Gas Slips as Corpus Christi Maintenance, Cameron Issues Weigh on Flows
items.summary: Feed gas nominations to Corpus Christi LNG could be limited through the end of the week during maintenance at a critical compressor station, according to Wood Mackenzie pipeline data.

items.title: SEFE’s Landmark Argentine LNG Deal Bolsters Europe Energy Security
items.summary: Germany’s Securing Energy for Europe (SEFE) signed a deal to buy up to 2 million tons/year (Mt/y) of LNG for eight years from Southern Energy SA in Argentina. The deal is Argentina’s first long-term LNG supply contract.

items.title: Futures Settle: Prices Pull Back as Natural Gas Market Navigates Fog of Iran War, Braces for Storage Print
items.summary: Natural gas futures traders hit the brakes on Wednesday as they awaited word on possible discussions to end the war in Iran, a conflict that injected fears of global supply shortages and sent prices higher to start the week.

items.title: MidDay Snapshot: Natural Gas Cash Pricing Mixed as Mild Weather Sets in
items.summary: Spot natural gas prices for day-ahead delivery varied by region on Wednesday, though overall demand was trending lower on widespread unseasonable warmth.

items.title: ‘Nightmare Scenario’ in Middle East as Qatari LNG Output Remains Offline With No End in Sight
items.summary: QatarEnergy’s decision this week to suspend LNG production after its infrastructure was attacked by Iran dealt a major blow to the global natural gas market, with practically no options to replace that supply and a slow process ahead for restarting o

items.title: Midmorning Markets: Natural Gas Cash Slides on ‘Spring Fever’ Warm-up
items.summary: Natural gas physical markets weakened Wednesday morning, with Henry Hub cash on track to fall to its lowest level since mid-January as forecasts call for a sharp drop in heating demand.

items.title: NextDecade Eyes Year-End Commissioning for First Rio Grande LNG Train
items.summary: Commissioning activities for the first train at Rio Grande LNG could begin by the end of the year as construction of the first phase tracks ahead of schedule, according to NextDecade Corp.

items.title: U.S. Crude Inventories Gain as Gasoline Inventories Keep Drawing
items.summary: Crude oil inventories in the United States increased by 3.5 million barrels during the week ending February 27, according to new data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) released on Wednesday. The increase brings commercial stockpil

items.title: QatarEnergy Declares Force Majeure After Halting LNG Production
items.summary: QatarEnergy has declared force majeure on liquefied natural gas (LNG) supplies after suspending production at key export facilities, sending fresh shockwaves through global energy markets already rattled by the escalating conflict involving Iran, the

items.title: Indian Refiner MRPL Declares Force Majeure on Gasoline Exports
items.summary: Indian state-run refiner Mangalore Refinery and Petrochemicals Limited (MRPL) has declared force majeure on all planned gasoline export cargoes for March and April as the Middle East war is halting crude supply out of the Gulf, Reuters reported on We

items.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Sends European Gas Benchmark Soaring
items.summary: Military strikes in the Middle East and the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz have triggered one of the sharpest moves in European gas markets over the past year, with the Title Transfer Facility (TTF) benchmark surging on disrupted liquefied
